Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties are ordered to appear. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: SHERRI WATSON VS. ROBERT G GISH M.D.
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 29, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER's AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off calendar. Duplicate entry. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHERRI WATSON VS. ROBERT G GISH M.D.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cpmc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 29, 2013, Line 21, Defendant CPMC'S Motion For Summary Judgment. Denied. California Pacific Medical Center has met its initial burden on the medical malpractice claim to show that its conduct fell within the community standard of care. (Munro v. Regents of University of California (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 984-85; see Austin Decl., Exhibits L, M.) Ms. Watson had met her shifting burden by coming forward with conflicting expert evidence. (Opp., Friedman Decl.) Summary judgment is denied, there is at least one triable issue of material fact. (CCP § 473c(g).) rulings.Case Title: CURTIS KEKOA JR. VS. GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 29, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Defendant has not met its initial burden of proof to show that the underlying Sagonowsky action does not rise out of the ownership of the motor vehicle. The insurance policy covers bodily injury "arising out of the ownership, maintenance or use of the owned auto or a non-owned auto." (Moura Decl., Exhibit A.) The cases cited by Geico are distinguishable from the instant action; they involve the definition of "use" and not "arising out of the ownership." Neither the policy's intentional injury exclusion nor Ins. Code § 533 pertain to the Ms. Sagonowsky's claim of negligence. " Despite the foregoing, we have nevertheless held that, in a "mixed" action, the insurer has a duty to defend the action in its entirety." (Buss v. Sup.Ct. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 35, 48.) Geico has not met its initial burden to show that a good faith dispute existed. The denial letter dated March 18, 2011 is insufficient to meet this threshold burden. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CURTIS KEKOA JR. VS. GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 29, 2013, Line 23, PLAINTIFF CURTIS KEKOA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted. Mr. Kekoa has met his initial burden to demonstrate that Geico had a duty to defend in the underlying action, Sagonowsky v. Kekoa. The duty to defend is broad, and it is the burden of the insured to bring the claim within the basic scope of coverage. (R.A. Stuchbery & Others Syndicate 1096 v. Redland Ins. Co. (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 796, 800-801.) The insurance policy covers bodily injury "arising out of the ownership, maintenance or use of the owned auto or a non-owned auto." (Plaintiff's Evidence, Exhibit A.) Mr. Kekoa has met his initial burden to show that the underlying personal injury action was a result over a dispute over ownership of the subject motor vehicle falls under the "arising out of the ownership" of the car. The cases cited by Geico in opposition are distinguishable from the instant action; they involve the definition of "use" and not "arising out of the ownership." Though the Sagonowsky Complaint does not assert any claim of ownership, Mr. Kekoa presented additional evidence to show a potential for coverage. "A defense is owed when the insurer has knowledge of facts showing a potential for coverage, even if the complaint fails to allege such facts." (Croskey, Cal. Practice Guide: Ins. Litigation (The Rutter Group) 7:584.) The ownership of the vehicle is not at issue. (UMF no. 14.) Geico has met its shifting burden. Therefore, the motion for summary adjudication on the issue of the duty to defend is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN CORNELL VS. SKYWEST, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 29, 2013, Line 24, DEFENDANTs SKYWEST AIRLINES, INC. DOING BUSINESS AS SKYWEST, BETH LOUDERMILK, BRIAN HANDSACKER, FREDERICK ASSILY, YING MEI, FANGAFUA TUIFUA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to cause of action 1 for negligence is overruled. The Court cannot, as a matter of law, determine on the face of the complaint that the FAA or ACAA has field preemption over Plaintiff's state law claim of negligence. See Gilstrap v. United Air Lines, Inc., (9th Cir. Mar. 12, 2013, WL 930170. Defendant's demurrer to cause of action 2 for negligent hiring, training, supervision, and retention is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead additional facts to establish a state common law standard of care. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiff shall email a courtesy copy of her opposition papers to the Judge Pro Tem, with a cc to opposing counsel, as soon as possible on March 28, 2013 at the following email address: tschoenberg@fbm.com. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING LLC VS. HAYDEE RUIZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 29, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Granted, with ten days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
